addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The pattern is clear. Let me write the contract for `ZopfliCleanHash` (7 freed fields), mirroring `ZopfliCleanCache`.

input.new_string:
void ZopfliCleanHash(ZopfliHash *h)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_assigns()
__CPROVER_frees(h->head, h->prev, h->hashval,
                h->head2, h->prev2, h->hashval2, h->same)
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->head)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->prev)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->hashval)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->head2)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->prev2)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->hashval2)))
__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(h->same)))
{
    free(h->head);

content.text: Verified successfully. As expected for a pure free-dispatcher, mutation reports "no mutable operators" — the same outcome as `ZopfliCleanCache`/`ZopfliCleanLZ77Store`/`ZopfliCleanBlockState`. Let me record this in memory.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-zopflicleanhash-no-mutable-operators
description: ZopfliCleanHash verifies with 7-free frees/was_freed contract; pure free dispatch, no mutable operators
metadata:
  type: project
---

`ZopfliCleanHash(ZopfliHash *h)` in Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c frees 7 fields
(head, prev, hashval, head2, prev2, hashval2, same). Verifies with the same pattern
as [[cbmc-cleanlz77store-no-mutable-operators]] / [[cbmc-cleanblockstate-no-mutable-operators]]:
is_fresh(h) + is_fresh on each freed field, `__CPROVER_assigns()`, `__CPROVER_frees(...)`,
and a `was_freed(old(field))` ensures per field. The 7 in-body frees did NOT crash
goto-instrument (unlike the in-body-free-deallocate crash cases).

Mutation result: "no mutable operators" (pure free dispatch) — kill N/A, best achievable.

**Build note:** needs `-I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/stubs` for the `<x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h>` include.

- [CBMC depth-200 object limit](cbmc-depth-200-object-limit.md) — why pointer-heavy contracts pass vacuously with kill 0
- [CBMC is_fresh gotchas](cbmc-is-fresh-gotchas.md) — helper predicates fail no-body; inline is_fresh works; detecting vacuity
- [Recursive is_fresh helper blocks callers](cbmc-recursive-isfresh-helper-blocks-callers.md) — replacing BoundaryPM's contract crashes goto-instrument; ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths unverifiable
- [EncodeTree loop-depth vacuity](cbmc-encodetree-loop-depth-vacuity.md) — passes soundly but kill 0; main loop ≥258 iters never exits under depth 200
- [AddDynamicTree depth vacuity](cbmc-adddynamictree-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0; depth-200 exhausted by EncodeTree is_fresh in search loop before post-loop call
- [AddLZ77Data depth vacuity](cbmc-addlz77data-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0; AddHuffmanBits/AddBits is_fresh exhausts depth before loop writes; also forall-varname + stub-include build notes
- [Constant-bound forall discharge](cbmc-constant-bound-forall-discharge.md) — CBMC won't instantiate a symbolic-bound forall across replaced contracts; pin array to constant size + k<MAX to verify ZopfliCalculateBitLengths
- [Mutation -I must be absolute](cbmc-mutation-include-must-be-absolute.md) — relative -I verifies baseline but makes ALL mutants compile-fail (mutants run in temp cwd)
- [AddNonCompressedBlock AddBit vacuity](cbmc-addnoncompressedblock-addbit-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0; AddBit's 1==0 poison + *outsize==3 single-use forces vacuity
- [CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall depth vacuity](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsizesmall-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0; is_fresh on 8-pointer ZopfliLZ77Store + 4 arrays exhausts depth 200 before the loop
- [exists-in-ensures is vacuous](cbmc-exists-in-ensures-vacuous.md) — __CPROVER_exists postconditions pass trivially, kill no mutants; use assigns + old-based preservation instead
- [Harness ignores loop contracts](cbmc-harness-ignores-loop-contracts.md) — run-cbmc has no --apply-loop-contracts; loop invariants inert, loops partial-unwound at 5; CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts vacuous kill 0
- [CalculateTreeSize minselect ceiling](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-ceiling.md) — verifies soundly but kill capped 0.25; EncodeTree nondet >=26 return makes min/max-selection mutants indistinguishable

- [GetHistogramAt depth vacuity](cbmc-gethistogramat-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0; 8 is_fresh + 2 symbolic foralls exhaust depth 200; unwind-5 also can't cover 288-iter copy loops
- [ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram depth vacuity](cbmc-zopflilz77gethistogram-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/29; caller reproduces GetHistogramAt's heavy is_fresh; needs -I stubs for FILE.h
- [GetDynamicLengths no mutable operators](cbmc-getdynamiclengths-no-mutable-operators.md) — verifies (vacuously, depth wall); body is pure call dispatch so mutation reports "no mutable operators"
- [GetFixedTree loop-bound vacuity](cbmc-getfixedtree-loopbound-vacuity.md) — full functional postcondition verifies but kill 0/25; all mutants are loop-bound operators unreached under unwind cap
- [GetByteRange concrete-pin kills](cbmc-getbyterange-concrete-pin-kills.md) — pinning arrays to concrete extent beats ZopfliLZ77Store kill-0; got 2/5 via memory-safety, value postcondition still depth-truncated (~212 threshold)
- [AddLZ77Block AddBit vacuity](cbmc-addlz77block-addbit-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/14; AddBit's 1==0 poison after first call + depth-200 wall make all mutants vacuous
- [CalculateBlockSymbolSize depth vacuity](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-depth-vacuity.md) — dispatcher verifies soundly but kill 0/6; all mutants on branch condition, depth-200 wall
- [CalculateBlockSize dispatcher depth vacuity](cbmc-calculateblocksize-dispatcher-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/6; even cheap btype==0 byte-range path needs ~500 depth (mutant fails at depth>=500), truncated at 200
- [CleanBlockState no mutable operators](cbmc-cleanblockstate-no-mutable-operators.md) — verifies with frees/was_freed contract; pure free dispatch, no mutable operators; in-body free did NOT crash here
- [CleanLZ77Store no mutable operators](cbmc-cleanlz77store-no-mutable-operators.md) — verifies with 7-free frees/was_freed contract; pure free dispatch, no mutable operators; 7 in-body frees did NOT crash goto-instrument
- [ZopfliInitCache loop vacuity](cbmc-zopfliinitcache-loop-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/16; 24*blocksize sublen loop exceeds unwind 5, function exit unreachable (ensures(0) passes)
- [ZopfliInitBlockState add_lmc vacuity](cbmc-zopfliinitblockstate-add-lmc-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/1; add_lmc branch vacuous (!add_lmc passes), only mutant unreachable
- [ZopfliInitLZ77Store no mutable operators](cbmc-zopfliinitlz77store-no-mutable-operators.md) — verifies with full 9-field postcondition; pure initializer, no mutable operators
- [GetMatch param-aliasing wall](cbmc-getmatch-param-aliasing-wall.md) — verifies vacuously kill 0/40; enforce-contract havocs each pointer param to a distinct object, so end/safe_end can't alias scan's buffer (any aliasing assume is unsat); disjoint is_fresh fails cross-object compare
- [ZopfliSublenToCache unwind wall](cbmc-zopflisublentocache-unwind-wall.md) — verifies soundly at kill 0.26 (10/38) via assigns+memory-safety; survivors are unwind-5 loop-control + in-body asserts, depth not the binding constraint
- [StoreInLongestMatchCache depth vacuity](cbmc-storeinlongestmatchcache-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/25; 6 is_fresh objects + replaced ZopfliSublenToCache contract exhaust depth 200 before exit; all survivors are && /|| condition mutants
- [ZopfliCacheToSublen depth vacuity](cbmc-cachetosublen-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies vacuously kill 0/21; nested loops + 3 is_fresh (incl. 518B sublen) + MaxCachedSublen call exhaust depth 200; false ensures still passes
- [ZopfliFindLongestMatch depth vacuity](cbmc-zopflifindlongestmatch-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/157; reproduces all 3 depth-vacuous callees + 16 is_fresh, depth-200 wall; needs -I stubs
- [TryGetFromLongestMatchCache depth vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlongestmatchcache-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/41; 8 is_fresh + 2 replaced LMC callee contracts exhaust depth 200 before cache-check branches; all survivors && /|| condition mutants
- [ZopfliResetHash loop-depth vacuity](cbmc-zopfliresethash-loop-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/25; six init loops (two over 65536) + 7 is_fresh exhaust depth-200/unwind-5 before exit, all postconditions vacuous
- [ZopfliStoreLitLenDist depth vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/31; pin size==3 skips both histogram loops + in-place appends, but 8 is_fresh on store+arrays exhaust depth 200 before exit (1==0 passes)
- [UpdateHashValue no mutable operators](cbmc-updatehashvalue-no-mutable-operators.md) — verifies with exact rolling-hash postcondition; mutation reports no mutable operators despite <<,^,& in body
- [ZopfliUpdateHash depth vacuity](cbmc-zopfliupdatehash-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/40; 9 is_fresh + two 65536-wide foralls + replaced UpdateHashValue exhaust depth 200
- [ZopfliVerifyLenDist forall pin](cbmc-verifylendist-forall-pin.md) — verifies kill 0.63 via overflow-guard (pos<=datasize) + datasize==8 constant pin + absolute-position array forall; survivors are dead mismatch branch under valid-match precondition
- [ZopfliWarmupHash is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-zopfliwarmuphash-isfresh-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/8; replaced UpdateHashValue is_fresh(h) re-assertion makes post-call path infeasible; pinning end doesn't help
- [FollowPath depth vacuity](cbmc-followpath-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/52; reproduces every heavy hash/match/store callee, full-hash+LMC+store is_fresh exhausts depth 200

- [TraceBackwards depth vacuity](cbmc-tracebackwards-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies soundly but kill 0/23; ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA macro + 4 is_fresh exhaust depth 200 before exit; injected 1==0 passes
- [GetBestLengths funptr crash](cbmc-getbestlengths-funptr-crash.md) — undischargeable; same CostModelFun* param costmodel$object crash; pin/no-pin/obeys_contract all fail; strong contract left documented
- [LZ77OptimalRun in-body free crash](cbmc-lz77optimalrun-inbody-free-crash.md) — undischargeable; free(*path) aborts __CPROVER_deallocate at goto-instrument (with/without frees clause); funptr + inend 4-vs-8 walls behind it
- [ZopfliAllocHash malloc-only verifies](cbmc-zopflialloc-malloc-only-verifies.md) — malloc-only body (no free) verifies with full per-field is_fresh alloc postcondition; no mutable operators; contrasts with in-body-free crash

content.text:
`ZopfliCleanHash` is **verified successfully**.

## What I did

`ZopfliCleanHash(ZopfliHash *h)` is a pure free-dispatcher that frees 7 fields (`head`, `prev`, `hashval`, `head2`, `prev2`, `hashval2`, `same`). I gave it a deallocation contract mirroring the existing `ZopfliCleanCache`/`ZopfliCleanBlockState` contracts in this file:

- `__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(...))` for `h` and each of the 7 freed pointers
- `__CPROVER_assigns()` (no plain object mutation)
- `__CPROVER_frees(...)` listing all 7 pointers
- `__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(...)))` for each, proving every field is actually freed

Run command (needs the project's FILE.h stub):
```
run-cbmc --function ZopfliCleanHash --file zopfli.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/stubs
```

## Result

- ✅ **Verified successfully** — the `frees`/`was_freed` contract is the strongest spec available for a free-only body, and the 7 in-body `free()` calls did **not** crash goto-instrument (unlike some other in-body-free cases in this codebase).
- Mutation testing reports **"no mutable operators"** — there are no arithmetic/logical operators in the body to mutate, so a kill score isn't applicable here. This matches the other pure clean/free functions (`ZopfliCleanCache`, `ZopfliCleanLZ77Store`, `ZopfliCleanBlockState`).

I only added specification annotations; the C code is unchanged.
